title: ListItem
description: A single item in a list.
$comment:
This is an implementation of schema.org
[`ListItem`](https://schema.org/ListItem), extended in Stencila Schema for
document-oriented list content.

In addition to the schema.org model, it supports block `content` for rich list
items and `isChecked` for task-list semantics. This makes it suitable for
ordinary prose lists as well as checklists and mixed-content document lists.

Key properties include `content`, `item`, `isChecked`, and `position`.

analogues.notes: Direct schema.org source type, extended in Stencila with rich block `content` and task-list state.
Closest HTML analogue for list items, though Stencila can also carry `isChecked`, `position`, and structured node-valued `item` metadata.
Closest JATS analogue for list items.
One common Pandoc representation for a simple list item containing inline-only content.
Pandoc models list items as nested block lists within list constructors rather than as standalone `ListItem` nodes.
Closest MDAST analogue; Stencila additionally supports schema.org-style item metadata and explicit checklist state.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

identifiers.description: Any kind of identifier for any kind of Thing.
identifiers.$comment:
Some identifiers have specific properties e.g the `issn` property for
the `Periodical` type. These should be used in preference to this
property which is intended for identifiers that do not yet have a
specific property. Identifiers can be represented as strings, but
using a `PropertyValue` will usually be better because it allows
for `propertyID` (i.e. the type of identifier).

content.description: The content of the list item.

high.description: Generate up to two, arbitrary, non-list blocks

max.description: Generate up to four, arbitrary, non-list blocks

item.description: The item represented by this list item.

isChecked.description: A flag to indicate if this list item is checked.

position.description: The position of the item in a series or sequence of items.
